main_question: Can the initial POCD module scoring workflow run successfully on a POCD hippocampus dataset?
module_focus: NAD_SIRT1; FAO; MITO_RESPIRATORY_CHAIN; OXIDATIVE_STRESS; INFLAMMATION; INSULIN_SIGNALING; TGF_BETA; CREB_NR2B_SYNAPSE

main_question: Are mitochondrial and respiratory-chain modules consistently altered in a PND-related surgery model?
Are inflammatory and NF-kB-related modules altered and potentially reversible in a PND-related model?
Can miRNA-level changes provide regulatory support for candidate POCD modules?
Which hippocampal cell types express candidate module genes and hub genes?

validation_question: Does GSE115440 independently reproduce the mitochondrial-inflammation module-level direction observed in GSE95426?
analysis_plan: Run independent RMA normalization, QC, limma Surgery_vs_Control, GSVA_or_ssGSEA module scoring, leave_one_out robustness, then compare module_score_directionality with GSE95426.
notes: Primary comparison is Surgery vs Control only; Surgery+Maresin1 is reserved as treatment_rescue auxiliary group and is not used in the main cross-dataset POCD validation.
